Description:
Ballistics of the Type 63 122mm howitzer firing the high-explosive fragmentation shell with full charge. Domestically developed, the pattern was accepted in 1966, employed as divisional artillery.

Charge design includes 2.79 kg of 13/1, split into 3 x 0.25 kg and 4 x 0.51 kg bags, plus 0.61 kg of 4/1 pyroxylin grains, in a single base charge bag, with 50 grams of black powder ignition charge, totalling 3.45 kg. With the full charge, develops 612 m/s from 2750 kgf/cm^2 (copper crusher gauge, converts to 302 MPa). Projectile travel 30.68dm, chamber volume is 7.653 L and 6.52 dm long.

Zone	Charge Bags			P.mcp	m.v.	Range
	Thick	Thin	Base	kgf/cm^2
	0.51kg	0.25kg	0.61kg		m/s	m
Full	4	3	1	2750	612	15170
No.1	3	3	1	?	553
No.2	2	3	1	?	494
No.3	1	3	1	?	432
No.4	0	3	1	?	364
No.5	0	2	1	?	328
No.6	0	1	1	?	290
No.7	0	0	1	550	250	3670 (20 degree elev)

Tube measures 4300mm inclusive of muzzle device. Chamber (breech to start of rifling) measures 642mm, recoil length measures 780-920 mm, extreme stop at 1040 mm. The gun could be traversed 29 degrees to either side once emplaced, elevation range is -2.5-63.5 degrees. Combat weight is 3.2 ton, marching weight is 3.25 tons.

References:
- 《国产火炮手册》兵器工业部第二管理局，1982
- 《火炮内弹道计算手册》王连荣，张佩勤，国防工业出版社，1987
